Clear import/export properties at the end of the test

This avoids the pollution of system properties that might lead to failures following tests.

issue_title: Missing constraints on unique attributes in JPA Map store
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

The JPA Map store will save attributes as a set, that is each value for a an attribute must occur only once. 

While this is ensured in Java, there is no constraint in the database for this, yet. Due to this, the bug #11666 was hard to analyze, as the database got corrupted. 

### Version

999-SNAPSHOT

### Expected behavior

A constraint should prevent a corrupted data model and follow-up problem.

### Actual behavior

No constraints exist, yet.

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Ensure that values of attributes are unique in the database

While this is already ensured on the Java level when using a Set, database inconsistencies as occurred with Hibernate could lead to follow-up problems that are hard to analyze (as seen in #11666).

Closes #11671

issue_title: HardcodedLDAPRoleStorageMapper and HardcodedLDAPGroupStorageMapper ignore composite roles when checking whether user has role (was KEYCLOAK-18308)
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

We have configured a composite realm role `composite` which contains a client role `client-role`. We assign the `composite` to all LDAP users via the `HardcodedLDAPRoleStorageMapper`.

We have a custom authenticator which checks user roles via `UserModel.hasRole(role)`. 

### Version

16.0.0

### Expected behavior

We would expect `UserModel.hasRole(role)` to return `true` instead of `false`. It should respect the composition of roles even if the role is hardcoded.

### Actual behavior

`UserModel.hasRole(role)` always returns `false` when the given `role` is `client-role`.
However, we can see the `composite role` as well as the `client-role` in the role mappings tab beeing shown as effective.

### How to Reproduce?

- Create a client-role `client-role`
- Create a composite realm role `composite`
  - mark as composite
  - add the client role `client-role`
- Add a LDAP user federation
- Add `HardcodedLDAPRoleStorageMapper` to the user federation
  - Map realm role `composite` to all users
- Configure an authentication flow with `ConditionalRoleAuthenticator`
  - check for the client role `client-role`

Condition is never met, though all LDAP users have the client role assigned via the composite realm role.

### Anything else?

Similar behaviour can be observed with `HardcodedLDAPGroupStorageMapper`.

This clones [KEYCLOAK-18308](https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-18308)
commit_message:
Evaluate composite roles for hardcoded LDAP roles/groups

Closes: 11771

see also KEYCLOAK-18308

issue_title: Configure Otp with manual url is not generating the url correctly when keycloak is behind a reverse proxy on an url like http://www.host.com/auth
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

We have a keycloak that is running on an url like http://www.host.com/auth

There is a reverse proxy that is redirecting every request on http://ip:another_port/auth on a docker that expose keycloak on http://ip:port/

The TotpBean is getting an uriBuilder in its contructor. This UriBuilder corresponds to the UriInfo where the request is arriving, it's not based on the keycloak configuration that is defining the correct host and path

### Version

17.0.0

### Expected behavior

The request generated by the TotpBean for the manual url should be http://www.host.com/auth/manualUrl

### Actual behavior

The request generated by the TotpBean for the manual url is http://host.com/manualUrl

### How to Reproduce?

1 - Use a reverse proxy like apache or nginx
2 - Make Your keycloak run on ip:port/
3 - Reverse proxy the url on ip:another_port/auth on your keycloak that is running ip:port/
4 - Go to the OTP configuration page
5 - Try to switch on manual URL, the url is pointing on /manualUrl and not on /auth/manualUrl

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Make sure there is always Realm or ResourceServer when searching for authz entities
issue_body:
### Description

Currently, for authz store methods e.g. [here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authorization/store/PermissionTicketStore.java#L44) it is possible to set `ResourceServer` argument to `null`. This results in search across all realms and resource servers. We need to avoid this because it can be highly ineffective. 

We need to make sure all methods either have `RealmModel` attribute or cannot be called with `ResourceServer` == `null`

issue_title: Frontend url from wrong realm used in password update mails from admin panel
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

After upgrading our keycloak version to `17.0.0`, we couldn't send out correct `Update Password` mails from the admin panel. The reset URL in the mail pointed to an internal domain instead of our client facing domain.

After a long debug session we found that the `frontendUrl` realm setting in our master realm was used instead of the the `frontendUrl` for the specific realm. We believe it doesn't necessarily picks the master realm URL, but rather the `frontendUrl` from the realm where the admin is logged in to. We hot-fixed the problem by unsetting the `frontendUrl` in the master realm, but we expected it to work as it did before.

### Version

17.0.0/18.0.0

### Expected behavior

When an admin user resets a password for a user in another realm, we expect the `frontendUrl` for that specific realm to be used in the password reset mail, so the user will land on the correct realm when resetting his/her password.

### Actual behavior

The master realm `frontendUrl` is used instead for the password reset mail, when resetting a password from the master realm.

### How to Reproduce?

* Setup a clean keycloak instance (we used the `quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:18.0.0` image in a docker-compose setup, with postgres as DB) 
* Configure the `frontendUrl` of the master realm to the actual URL of the master (i.e. the URL from the address bar) in realm settings
* Add a new realm
  * Configure some arbitrary URL for `frontendUrl` under realm settings (e.g. `https://some.random.domain`)
  * Configure realm mail settings to point to your development mail server
  * Create a new user with a usable email address
* Go to the `Credentials` tab under the user
  * At the bottom of the page, set `Reset Actions` to `Update Password (UPDATE_PASSWORD)`
  * Hit `Send email`
* Check  your mailbox, there should be a reset link pointing to the master realm `frontendUrl` instead of the new realm's `frontendUrl`

### Anything else?

The problem occurred for us in both keycloak 17.0.0 and 18.0.0 (the quarkus version of both versions), but we only tried to reproduce for version 18.0.0.

issue_title: Implementation of OAuth2 authentication with HTTP Basic authentication scheme not RFC6749 compliant
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When authenticating to the keycloak oauth2 server with ` grant_type: "client_credentials"` and the HTTP Basic authentication scheme as described in [RFC6749 section 2.3.1](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-2.3.1) with url encoded special characters, keycloak rejects the authentication request with an 401 error response: `{
    "error": "unauthorized_client",
    "error_description": "Invalid client secret"
}`

### Version

16.1.1

### Expected behavior

Keycloak should decode provided credentials in a basic authentication header before validating their correctness as specified in RFC6749 standard.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak does not url decode the client credentials according to specification in basic authentication headers when authenticating an oauth2 client. Therefore requests of compliant clients with secrets containing special characters are not authenticated. 

### How to Reproduce?

When authenticating a client with `client_id = "user"` and `client_secret = "secret/with=special?character"`, a compliant authentication request with basic auth header would look like this:

`
POST /user-service/auth/realms/generic/protocol/openid-connect/token HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded;charset=UTF-8
Authorization: Basic dXNlcjpzZWNyZXQlMkZ3aXRoJTNEc3BlY2lhbCUzRmNoYXJhY3Rlcg==
User-Agent: PostmanRuntime/7.29.0
Accept: */*
Cache-Control: no-cache
Host: keycloak-test.testdomain.de
Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate, br
Connection: keep-alive
 
grant_type=client_credentials`

but gets denied with:
`
HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized
Cache-Control: no-store
X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block
Pragma: no-cache
X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN
Referrer-Policy: no-referrer
Date: Mon, 09 May 2022 13:13:54 GMT
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
Content-Length: 75
Content-Type: application/json
Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000;includeSubDomains
 
{"error":"unauthorized_client","error_description":"Invalid client secret"}`


### Anything else?

This issue has been brouht to our attantion through an update to the spring oauth2 client impementation: https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-security/issues/9610
commit_message:
Use base64 url decoded for client secret when authenticating with Basic Auth (#12486)

Closes #11908

issue_title: Keycloak does not support POST method mentioned in latest OIDC RP-Initiated Logout specs draft
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

OpenID Connect RP-Initiated Logout specifies that "OpenID Providers MUST support the use of the HTTP **GET and POST** methods defined in [RFC 7231](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-rpinitiated-1_0.html#RFC7231) [RFC7231] at the Logout Endpoint." : https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-rpinitiated-1_0.html#RPLogout

But Keycloak only supports GET method for the official OIDC Logout enpoint specification. 

The POST method of the logout endpoint requires client authentication and a refresh token which is not compliant with the OIDC specification.

### Version

Keycloak 18.0.0

### Expected behavior

The Logout Endpoint complies with OIDC RP-Initiated Logout specification when using POST method

### Actual behavior

The Logout Endpoint doe not comply with OIDC RP-Initiated Logout specification when using POST method

### How to Reproduce?

1) Authenticate on Keycloak to get a valid ID Token
2) POST the following request : 
curl -X POST --location "http://127.0.0.1:8080/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/logout" \
    -d "id_token_hint=<your_id_token>"

You get a response : 

HTTP/1.1 400 Bad Request
Referrer-Policy: no-referrer
X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN
Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block
Content-Type: application/json
content-length: 75

{
  "error": "invalid_client",
  "error_description": "Invalid client credentials"
}



### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Wrong order of stylesheets in Account-Theme (keycloak.v2)
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When extending the existing **account** theme it is unnecessary hard to override styles.

This is the code from the default  `index.ftl`:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/25704193/168092056-6b6b8d2b-18c0-47c0-b649-f493cc4504fe.png)

When adding custom styles in the properties file: e.g.`styles=css/account.css` these styles get applied **BEFORE** patternfly styles and `layout.css`. Which means you need to use either `!important`or increase the specificity for every selector you want to override. Both options are bad practice for styling things.



### Version

18.0.0

### Expected behavior

Specific styles should be added after the inherited styles. This is common practice in CSS.

### Actual behavior

The selectors in `layout.css` get applied, when they have the same specificity. 

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a new account-theme. And enable this theme for "account". 
2. Add the following `theme.properties`:

`parent=keycloak.v2`
`import=common/keycloak`
`styles=css/account.css`

3. Add a new stylesheet under `mytheme/css/account.css`.
4. Add any style with the same specificity as existing ones. E.g.:

`body {
    font-family: "Comic Sans", serif;
}`

5. Reload the account-page. See that the changes from `account.css` are overridden by the default styles.


### Anything else?

_No response_

### Describe the bug

When using a hostname that has no `A` record, but `AAAA` records, Keycloak will fail to boot with `org.postgresql.util.PSQLException`, caused by `java.net.UnknownHostException`.

Workaround is to set `JAVA_OPTS` to exclude `-Djava.net.preferIPv4Stack=true`, see #10482

### Version

18.0.0

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Actual behavior

issue_title: Negative refresh token expiration (exp timestamp in the past)
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

I came across a strage behavior (seemingly a bug) regarding the refresh token expiration.

Under some (unknown) circumstances, the refresh_token issued by Keycloak contains an `exp` claim timestamp that is **before** the `iat` timestamp, e.g.:

```
  "exp": 1652513276,
  "iat": 1652529209,
```

This happens when retrieving an access token + refresh token from the https://kc-host/realms/my-realm/protocol/openid-connect/token endpoint using the `authorization_code` grant type.

Likewise, the response from the token endpoint contains a negative `refresh_expires_in` value:

```
{
   "access_token": "eyJhbGciOiJ...",
   "expires_in": 300,
   "refresh_expires_in": -15933,
   "refresh_token": "eyJhbGciOiJ...",
   "token_type": "Bearer",
   "id_token":" eyJhbGciOiJ...",
   "not-before-policy": 0,
   "session_state": "fa8347b3-4717-4bd6-a061-99f087aebf7e",
   "scope": "openid"
}
```

Other timestamps/expirations are fine however (e.g., access token and KEYCLOAK_IDENTITY cookie in the same request showed OK).

I stumbled across this issue by recognizing a frequent "hard" refresh of my frontend client, since the regular refresh of the access token fails (due to the invalid refresh token, obviously). This led me to investigating said problem with the refesh token.

Noteworthy:
* When logging out of the Keycloak session completely,  the problem is gone on next login (exp timestamp with proper offset in the future)
* We use an IdP during login (Azure AD)

### Version

17.0.1

### Expected behavior

Refresh token expiration to be positive and as configured, e.g.:
`"refresh_expires_in": 1800`

### Actual behavior

Refresh token expiration is negative, e.g.:
`"refresh_expires_in": -15933`

### How to Reproduce?

Token lifespan relevant realm config:

```
  "revokeRefreshToken": false,
  "refreshTokenMaxReuse": 0,
  "accessTokenLifespan": 300,
  "accessTokenLifespanForImplicitFlow": 900,
  "ssoSessionIdleTimeout": 43200,
  "ssoSessionMaxLifespan": 86400,
  "ssoSessionIdleTimeoutRememberMe": 0,
  "ssoSessionMaxLifespanRememberMe": 0,
  "offlineSessionIdleTimeout": 2592000,
  "offlineSessionMaxLifespanEnabled": false,
  "offlineSessionMaxLifespan": 5184000,
  "clientSessionIdleTimeout": 1800,
  "clientSessionMaxLifespan": 36000,
  "clientOfflineSessionIdleTimeout": 0,
  "clientOfflineSessionMaxLifespan": 0,
  "accessCodeLifespan": 60,
  "accessCodeLifespanUserAction": 300,
  "accessCodeLifespanLogin": 1800,
```


Realm client config:

```json
    {
      "clientId": "my-client",
      "description": "",
      "rootUrl": "redacted",
      "adminUrl": "",
      "baseUrl": "/",
      "surrogateAuthRequired": false,
      "enabled": true,
      "alwaysDisplayInConsole": true,
      "clientAuthenticatorType": "client-secret",
      "redirectUris": redacted,
      "webOrigins": [
        "+"
      ],
      "notBefore": 0,
      "bearerOnly": false,
      "consentRequired": false,
      "standardFlowEnabled": true,
      "implicitFlowEnabled": false,
      "directAccessGrantsEnabled": false,
      "serviceAccountsEnabled": false,
      "publicClient": true,
      "frontchannelLogout": false,
      "protocol": "openid-connect",
      "attributes": {
        "saml.assertion.signature": "false",
        "saml.force.post.binding": "false",
        "saml.multivalued.roles": "false",
        "saml.encrypt": "false",
        "oauth2.device.authorization.grant.enabled": "false",
        "backchannel.logout.revoke.offline.tokens": "false",
        "saml.server.signature": "false",
        "saml.server.signature.keyinfo.ext": "false",
        "use.refresh.tokens": "true",
        "exclude.session.state.from.auth.response": "false",
        "oidc.ciba.grant.enabled": "false",
        "saml.artifact.binding": "false",
        "backchannel.logout.session.required": "true",
        "client_credentials.use_refresh_token": "false",
        "saml_force_name_id_format": "false",
        "saml.client.signature": "false",
        "tls.client.certificate.bound.access.tokens": "false",
        "saml.authnstatement": "false",
        "display.on.consent.screen": "false",
        "saml.onetimeuse.condition": "false"
      },
      "fullScopeAllowed": false,
      "nodeReRegistrationTimeout": -1
    },
```

Client application:
* Chrome on macOS

### Anything else?

Interestingly, until now, I only happened to experience this problem with Chrome on macOS. No issue so far on Chrome on Windows.
On the macOS machine, however, it also does not occur consistently. We have multiple Keycloak instances (for different environments), with the exact same Keycloak deployed (17.0.1) and identical realm configurations. On some instances I face the problem, while others are fine. Also, the problem is not always present.

I somewhat feel like there's a correlation that this problem occurs when I continue using the affected web frontend after the macbook was on standby/hibernate for a couple of hours.

issue_title: NPE in HardcodedUserSessionAttributeMapper on Token Exchange
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

(migrated from https://issues.redhat.com/browse/KEYCLOAK-16547)

Hardcoded User Session Attribute mapper in a Keycloak OpenID Connect IdP causes NPE when doing an (external to internal) token exchange (see below stacktrace).

The NPE occurs in `HardcodedUserSessionAttributeMapper#setHardcodedUserSessionAttribute` in this line:

`context.getAuthenticationSession().setUserSessionNote(attribute, attributeValue);`

where `context.getAuthenticationSession()` returns null.

Is it expected that there is no authentication session during token exchange? If yes, shouldn't there be a null check and does this mean user session attributes cannot be set in token exchange?

A login through the IdP works fine and the user session attribute is set.

A workaround is to use a Hardcoded Attribute mapper instead which of course means the attribute is stored along with the user.

```
2022-05-15 15:59:42,631 WARN [org.keycloak.events] (executor-thread-30) type=TOKEN_EXCHANGE_ERROR, realmId=luniks, clientId=luniks-public, userId=null, ipAddress=192.168.122.1, error=invalid_token, reason='user info call failure', auth_method=token_exchange, grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:token-exchange, subject_issuer=https://keycloak.luniks.net/realms/test, validation_method='user info', client_auth_method=client-secret
2022-05-15 16:00:05,603 ERROR [org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler] (executor-thread-30) Uncaught server error: java.lang.NullPointerException
at org.keycloak.broker.provider.HardcodedUserSessionAttributeMapper.setHardcodedUserSessionAttribute(HardcodedUserSessionAttributeMapper.java:117)
at org.keycloak.broker.provider.HardcodedUserSessionAttributeMapper.preprocessFederatedIdentity(HardcodedUserSessionAttributeMapper.java:96)
at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.DefaultTokenExchangeProvider.importUserFromExternalIdentity(DefaultTokenExchangeProvider.java:519)
at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.DefaultTokenExchangeProvider.exchangeExternalToken(DefaultTokenExchangeProvider.java:491)
at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.DefaultTokenExchangeProvider.tokenExchange(DefaultTokenExchangeProvider.java:157)
at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.DefaultTokenExchangeProvider.exchange(DefaultTokenExchangeProvider.java:123)
at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.TokenEndpoint.tokenExchange(TokenEndpoint.java:789)
at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.TokenEndpoint.processGrantRequest(TokenEndpoint.java:204)
at jdk.internal.reflect.GeneratedMethodAccessor415.invoke(Unknown Source)
```


### Version

18.0.0

### Expected behavior

Token exchange should succeed.

### Actual behavior

Token exchange fails with 500 Internal Server Error
{
	"error": "unknown_error"
}

Please see stacktrace in description.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Set up a Keycloak OpenID Connect identity provider
2. Create a Hardcoded User Session Attribute mapper
3. Log in through the IdP: OK
4. Make an external to internal token exchange: NPE

### Anything else?

[Pedro Igor Craveiro] added a comment - [2021/01/04 10:21 AM]

Yes, that mapper will never work because it relies on the authentication session. When doing external to internal exchange there is no authentication session (but only at the end when finally creating the tokens).

This is a bug and we need to fix it, otherwise, people won't be able to set session notes when using this form of exchange. So a simple check if null is probably not enough.

The `BrokeredIdentityContext` relies on an `AuthenticationSession` to set session notes to a user session when generating tokens, where the authentication session is only created at the end of the process when actually generating tokens.

IMO, a proper fix would be to change `BrokeredIdentityContext`to support this scenario and allow setting session notes even when there is no authentication session.

[Pedro Igor Craveiro] added a comment - [2021/02/02 3:40 PM]

Token exchange is going through a review in order to come up with an implementation more aligned with the specs as well as better support for other use cases. Please, take a look at https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak-community/pull/213.

Meanwhile, we are going to keep this one open so that we can consider it during the design.

NPE in HardcodedUserSessionAttributeMapper on Token Exchange
Closes #11996

Signed-off-by: Douglas Palmer <dpalmer@redhat.com>

```

	at org.keycloak.models.cache.infinispan.RoleAdapter.lambda$getCompositesStream$0(RoleAdapter.java:137)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ReferencePipeline$3$1.accept(ReferencePipeline.java:195)
	at java.base/java.util.HashMap$KeySpliterator.forEachRemaining(HashMap.java:1621)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.copyInto(AbstractPipeline.java:484)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.wrapAndCopyInto(AbstractPipeline.java:474)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ReduceOps$ReduceOp.evaluateSequential(ReduceOps.java:913)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.evaluate(AbstractPipeline.java:234)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ReferencePipeline.collect(ReferencePipeline.java:578)
	at org.keycloak.models.cache.infinispan.RoleAdapter.getCompositesStream(RoleAdapter.java:140)
	at org.keycloak.models.utils.RoleUtils.expandCompositeRolesStream(RoleUtils.java:179)
	at org.keycloak.models.utils.RoleUtils.lambda$expandCompositeRoles$6(RoleUtils.java:201)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ReferencePipeline$7$1.accept(ReferencePipeline.java:271)
```

### Version

999-SNAPSHOT

### Expected behavior

When a cache inconsistency is detected, the code should fall back on the delegate like for example in UserAdapter. 

### Actual behavior

Exception being thrown.

Run CreateDeleteRealms scenario in keycloak-benchmark

issue_title: Running the operator watching a different namespace fails to delete ingresses
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When running the Keycloak Operator watching multiple namespaces ( ref. #11938 ) if an Ingress gets disabled the operator fails to perform the operation.

### Version

18.0.0

### Expected behavior

The operator should correctly delete the ingress in the correct namespace.

### Actual behavior

The operator throws Exceptions:

```
2022-05-17 10:59:59,495 ERROR [io.jav.ope.pro.eve.ReconciliationDispatcher] (EventHandler-keycloakcontroller) Error during event processing ExecutionScope{ resource id: CustomResourceID{name='keycloak-XXX', namespace='keycloak-XXX'}, version: 8671327} failed.: io.fabric8.kubernetes.client.KubernetesClientException: Namespace mismatch. Item namespace:keycloak-XXX. Operation namespace:keycloak-system.
	at io.fabric8.kubernetes.client.dsl.base.OperationSupport.checkNamespace(OperationSupport.java:234)
	at io.fabric8.kubernetes.client.dsl.base.BaseOperation.delete(BaseOperation.java:486)
	at io.fabric8.kubernetes.client.dsl.base.BaseOperation.delete(BaseOperation.java:469)
	at io.fabric8.kubernetes.client.dsl.base.BaseOperation.delete(BaseOperation.java:83)
	at org.keycloak.operator.v2alpha1.KeycloakIngress.getReconciledResource(KeycloakIngress.java:47)
	at org.keycloak.operator.OperatorManagedResource.createOrUpdateReconciled(OperatorManagedResource.java:50)
	at org.keycloak.operator.v2alpha1.KeycloakController.reconcile(KeycloakController.java:123)
	at org.keycloak.operator.v2alpha1.KeycloakController.reconcile(KeycloakController.java:51)
	at org.keycloak.operator.v2alpha1.KeycloakController_ClientProxy.reconcile(Unknown Source)
	at io.javaoperatorsdk.operator.processing.Controller$2.execute(Controller.java:101)
	at io.javaoperatorsdk.operator.processing.Controller$2.execute(Controller.java:76)
	at io.javaoperatorsdk.operator.api.monitoring.Metrics.timeControllerExecution(Metrics.java:34)
	at io.javaoperatorsdk.operator.processing.Controller.reconcile(Controller.java:75)
	at io.javaoperatorsdk.operator.processing.event.ReconciliationDispatcher.reconcileExecution(ReconciliationDispatcher.java:143)
	at io.javaoperatorsdk.operator.processing.event.ReconciliationDispatcher.handleReconcile(ReconciliationDispatcher.java:109)
	at io.javaoperatorsdk.operator.processing.event.ReconciliationDispatcher.handleDispatch(ReconciliationDispatcher.java:74)
	at io.javaoperatorsdk.operator.processing.event.ReconciliationDispatcher.handleExecution(ReconciliationDispatcher.java:50)
	at io.javaoperatorsdk.operator.processing.event.EventProcessor$ControllerExecution.run(EventProcessor.java:349)
	at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor.runWorker(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:1128)
	at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:628)
	at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:829)

2022-05-17 10:59:59,496 ERROR [io.jav.ope.pro.eve.EventProcessor] (EventHandler-keycloakcontroller) Exhausted retries for ExecutionScope{ resource id: CustomResourceID{name='keycloak-XXX', namespace='keycloak-XXX'}, version: 8671327}
```


### How to Reproduce?

- Deploy the operator watching 2 different namespaces
- deploy two Keycloak CRs
- change one of them to disable the Ingress ( `disableDefaultIngress: true` )
- the operator crashes

### Anything else?

The issue is caused by the incorrect `namespace` getting picked up by the `fabric8` `kubernetes-client` configuration when not explicitly set, the fix, in this case, is to change this line:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/075e28445582f7bcd749c4d5daa012afb31a4561/operator/src/main/java/org/keycloak/operator/v2alpha1/KeycloakIngress.java#L47

to be:
```java
client.network().v1().ingresses().inNamespace(getNamespace()).delete(existingIngress);
```

Along with fixing this issue we should review all the `fabric8` `kubernetes-client` calls to explicitly set the target namespace to be sure we are not hitting more of those edge cases.

cc. @vmuzikar

issue_title: Account REST endpoint does not respect view permission settings from user profile on lastName and firstName
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

When the feature *declarative user profile* is enabled, it's possible to configure permissions to user attributes - whether the user or admin can view or edit specific attributes.

The account rest endpoint does not respect this setting for `lastName` and `firstName` - they are always shown to the user, regardless what is configured in the user profile. 

Even though this only affects some very special and probably rare use cases, the behavior is not consistent and should be fixed.

### Version

18.0

### Expected behavior

When the user profile does not allow view access to *firstName* and/or *lastName*, the account rest endpoint should not provide these attributes. The account UI should not shown them.

### Actual behavior

The attributes `firstName` and/or `lastName` are always returned by the account rest endpoint, regardless of the permissions configured in the user profile.

### How to Reproduce?

* enable declarative user profile by setting `-Dkeycloak.profile.feature.declarative_user_profile=enabled`
* remove view permission for user on `lastName` and `firstName`, e.g. like this: 
```
...
```

issue_title: IdP Mappers ignored when performing external -> internal token exchange
issue_body: ### Describe the bug Keycloak_A has Keycloak_B configured as Identity Provider (IdP). I configured 3 mappers, see attached screenshots. Only the "UserName Template Importer" of mapper category Preprocessor seems to work - all attribute importers, hardcoded, or based on claims in the IdP user info are ignored. The exact same mappers work when the user is created by regular login on Keycloak_A using the Keycloak_B as IdP. In my case I am using the mappers to avoid potential conflict between multiple IdPs to guarantee unique user names through the user name mapper but I also plan to ignore the IdP's email to respect the unicity constraints on the email address. ### Version 16.1.1 ### Expected behavior I expect the mappers to work during token exchange just as they would during regular user creation/login through the IdP. ### Actual behavior Mappers of type Attribute Importer/Hardcoded Attribute are ignored. ### How to Reproduce? 1. On a Keycloak instance A configure an IdP which is Keycloak instance B. 2. Configure a Hardcoded Attribute mapper on the IdP which maps email to empty - see one of the attached screenshots. 3. Create a user with an email address on Keycloak B. 4. On Keycloak A login the user through the IdP using for instance the account client. 5. Verify the user has no email attribute. 6. Delete the user on Keycloak A 7. Generate a token for the user directly on Keycloak B. 8. Do token exchange against Keycloak A using the token from the previous step. 9. Verify that email attribute on user has (erroneously) been set. curl used for token exchange: ``` curl --location --request POST 'http://localhost:8080/auth/realms/myrealm/protocol/openid-connect/token' \ --header "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \ --data-urlencode "grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:token-exchange" \ --data-urlencode "subject_token_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:token-type:access_token" \ --data-urlencode "client_id=token-exchange-test" \ --data-urlencode "subject_token=${token}" \ --data-urlencode "subject_issuer=${idpName}" ``` ### Anything else? The documentation mentions that token exchange can only work when a user to be imported through the IdP does not exist yet - where I imagine "does not exist" means user name and email as provided by the IdP do not match an existing user. In my case I created a specific realm for token exchange - and I prefix user names with the IdP name as to make sure that this unicity is guaranteed. Stripping the email is the 2nd required element for this to work for my use case which consists of adding multiple IdPs which I do not control and which may have an overlap on user names and email addresses. I'd appreciate any work-around on this issue.

issue_title: IDP with PKCE: Token-Request body contains non-standard parameter
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

We are configuring a IdentityProvider in Keycloak with PKCE enabled and PKCE method "SH256". In the token request Keycloak sends the parameter "code_challenge_method" although it's non-standard.  The Identity-Provider we configure cancels the request because the request body contains a non-standard parameter. For security reasons all requests with non-standard parameters are blocked.

Specs: 
- https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#TokenRequest
- https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7636#section-6.1


Can the parameter "code_challenge_method" be removed in one of the next keycloak versions?

### Version

18.0.0

### Expected behavior

Token Request should not contain parameter "code_challenge_method"

 ```
 POST /token HTTP/1.1
Host: server.example.com
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Authorization: Basic czZCaGRSa3F0MzpnWDFmQmF0M2JW

grant_type=authorization_code
&code=SplxlOBeZQQYbYS6WxSbIA
&redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fclient.example.org%2Fcb
&code_verifier=random_code_verifier
```

### Actual behavior

Token Request contains parameter "code_challenge_method"

 ```
 POST /token HTTP/1.1
Host: server.example.com
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Authorization: Basic czZCaGRSa3F0MzpnWDFmQmF0M2JW

grant_type=authorization_code
&code=SplxlOBeZQQYbYS6WxSbIA
&redirect_uri=https%3A%2F%2Fclient.example.org%2Fcb
&code_verifier=random_code_verifier
&code_challenge_method=S256
```

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Remove non-standard `code_challenge_method` from token request for IDP (#12473)

Closes #12141

issue_title: Avoiding unnecessary roundtrips to the database when evaluating permissions
issue_body:
### Describe the bug

The recent changes to refactor the authorization store introduced a performance degradation when evaluating permissions. Basically, unnecessary database queries are being executed even though the data being queried is available in the cache.

There is also an unnecessary query being executed when querying for client scopes where if the scope is the client itself we should just return the client instead of executing a database query.

### Version

17.0.1

### Expected behavior

When evaluating permissions database queries should not happen if the cache is hot.

### Actual behavior

Unnecessary queries are being run even though the cache is hot.

### How to Reproduce?

* Enable logging to log database queries
* Create a client and enable authorization services
* Request an access token using the `urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:uma-ticket`

You should see unnecessary queries to fetch client scopes, resource server, and associated policies even though entries are cached after the first run.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Avoiding unnecessary roundtrips to the database when evaluating permissions

Closes #12148
